refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain analysis, obstacle analysis, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, avoiding any implementation details or combined requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate an accurate analysis of the terrain features of the worksite area, independent of obstacles or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate safety and operational policy constraints into the terrain analysis
Provide an interface to share the generated worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area

potential_sources: Integration of safety and operational policies tightly coupled with terrain modeling
Sharing interface dependent on specific planning/execution function implementations
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a digital terrain model, which is a logical and independent solution concept. Other candidate DPs such as integrating safety policies or providing interfaces are better treated as separate DPs or implementation concerns to maintain independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to generate an accurate terrain model
Risk that safety and operational policies may require frequent updates, potentially impacting model consistency
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the generated model in agreed formats
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing the FR further to separate terrain modeling from policy integration and interface provisioning to ensure clear independence and modularity in DPs.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions related to the worksite, including generation of digital terrain models.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle and other sources to build the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands or requests related to terrain model generation or updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems or remote operators for terrain data sharing and updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle mainly provides raw data; implementing terrain modeling here would cause high diffusion and coupling.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not data modeling; poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services are unrelated to terrain modeling; containment is very weak.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the digital terrain model generation DP because it centralizes data processing and terrain-related functions, minimizing diffusion and coupling with other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module.
Network Link interface may introduce latency or data consistency issues affecting terrain model updates.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain' by producing a digital representation of the terrain, which is a clear and verifiable functional decomposition.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on telemetry data from the Vehicle module may cause delays or inaccuracies if data is not timely or accurate.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1) for the terrain modeling DP, indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of responsibilities. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through acceptance criteria requiring the generated worksite model/map to be available to planning and execution functions, which can be tested via system integration and functional tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the DP within a single module boundary, ensuring clear allocation and responsibility for implementation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate risks related to telemetry data dependency to ensure timely and accurate terrain model updates.
Address potential latency and data consistency issues introduced by the Network Link interface to maintain model integrity.
Consider defining explicit interface contracts and data validation mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Network Link modules to reduce integration risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear verification feasibility, strong containment, and clear allocation of responsibilities. The DP directly satisfies the FR with a verifiable functional decomposition, and the implementation module boundary is well defined with minimal diffusion risks. Although there are some integration risks related to telemetry data dependency and network link latency, these are manageable through monitoring and mitigation rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain a clear and stable design at this level rather than complicate the hierarchy unnecessarily.

revise.why: To address integration risks, revise the implementation module interfaces by defining explicit interface contracts and data validation mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Network Link modules to reduce integration risks and ensure timely and accurate terrain model updates.

assumptions: Telemetry data from Vehicle module is generally reliable but may have occasional delays or inaccuracies.
Network Link interface may introduce latency and data consistency issues that can affect model integrity.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data causing delays or inaccuracies in terrain model updates.
Potential latency and data consistency issues from Network Link interface impacting model integrity.
evidence_needed: System integration and functional test results verifying the availability and accuracy of the generated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions.
Verification of interface contracts and data validation mechanisms effectiveness in mitigating integration risks.
